mnemonics.title: Remember Population vs Sample
mnemonics.content: PPSS: POPULATION = PARAMETER (fixed, unknown), SAMPLE = STATISTIC (varies, known). PARAMETER and POPULATION both start with P.

mnemonics.title: Remember types of inference
mnemonics.content: INFER: I=N for Estimation, N=H for Hypothesis testing. ESTIMATE the parameter, TEST the hypothesis.

mnemonics.title: Remember CLT
mnemonics.content: CLT: Central Limit Theorem - Large Sample = Normal Distribution regardless of population shape.

mnemonics.title: Remember Hypothesis Testing terms
mnemonics.content: H0 = NOTHING (null hypothesis - no effect), H1 = INTERESTING (alternative - what we hope to prove).

acronyms.usage: Measures variability of sample statistic across samples. SE = σ/√n for sample mean.
Sampling distribution approaches normal as sample size increases.
Sample statistic converges to population parameter as n increases.
Range of plausible values for unknown parameter at given confidence level.
Default assumption of no effect or relationship being tested.
Research hypothesis that contradicts null hypothesis.
Each observation is independent and comes from same probability distribution.
Method to estimate parameters by maximizing the likelihood function.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a large circle representing population, with many smaller circles inside representing samples. The population is the whole, samples are parts we actually observe.
Imagine taking 1000 different samples from same population, computing mean for each, then plotting histogram of these 1000 means - that's the sampling distribution.
Think of CI as a fishing net casting into water - we're trying to 'catch' the unknown parameter. Higher confidence = wider net.
H0 = defendant is innocent (no effect). We collect evidence (data). If evidence is strong enough beyond reasonable doubt (p < α), we reject innocence.
Population has spread (σ). SE is like a zoomed-in view showing where the sample mean likely falls - smaller SE means more precise estimate.

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant tasting soup - you taste one spoonful (sample) and infer the taste of the entire pot (population). One spoonful cannot represent everything perfectly, but gives reasonable idea.
Parameter is like the exact weight of all students in DU (unknown truth). Statistic is like average weight of 100 students you measured (your estimate).
Imagine measuring height of 100 different groups of 30 students each. Each group has different average. The distribution of these group averages is the sampling distribution.
Weather forecast says 20-25°C. You don't know exact temperature but you're 90% confident it's in this range. More confidence = wider range.
Throwing darts at target. Population is center of target. Each dart throw (sample) lands around center. SE measures how spread out the dart hits are - small SE = accurate throwing.
